{
  "title": "How to File Your Irish Taxes Online in 2025",
  "excerpt": "A comprehensive step-by-step guide to filing your Irish taxes online for the 2025 tax year with helpful tips and common pitfalls to avoid.",
  "content": "Filing your taxes online in Ireland has become more streamlined than ever before. The Revenue Online Service (ROS) provides a user-friendly platform that makes the entire process straightforward. In this comprehensive guide, we'll walk you through each step of the process, from gathering your essential documents to successfully submitting your return.\n\n## Getting Started\n\nBefore you begin the online filing process, ensure you have all necessary documents ready. This includes your P60, P45s, medical receipts, and any other relevant tax documents...",
  "imageAlt": "Person using laptop to file Irish taxes online with Revenue website visible on screen",
  "status": true,
  "isFeatured": true,
  "featuredOrder": 1
}

{
    "name": "Working Family Payment",
    "description": "A weekly payment for employees and the self-employed with children.",
    "details": "Must be working at least 19 hours per week and have dependent children. Apply online through MyWelfare.ie or visit your local Intreo office. P60, payslips, birth certificates of children, bank details.",
    "icon":"https://api.taxmind.ie/wp?key=website/taxCredit/1736923625686mJZds32Dr.png",
    "status": true
}

{
    "name": "Dependent Relative Tax Credit",
    "description": "Receive €245 per dependent annually for supporting a dependent relative",
    "details": "The Dependent Relative Tax Credit is a tax credit available in Ireland to individuals who maintain a dependent relative. This credit is designed to provide financial relief to those who support a dependent family memberWho Qualifies as a Dependent Relative?A dependent relative includes:A relative, or a relative of your spouse or civil partner, who is incapacitated by old age or illness.A widowed parent, or a parent who is a surviving civil partner, regardless of their income level.A child of the claimant, or of the claimant’s spouse or civil partner, who is permanently incapacitated due to physical or mental disability.Amount of the Tax CreditThe Dependent Relative Tax Credit for 2024 is €245 and 2025 €305This amount is non-refundable, meaning it can only be used to reduce your tax liability and cannot be claimed as a cash refund.Claim it by apply now ",
    "icon":"https://api.taxmind.ie/wp?key=website/taxCredit/1736923625686mJZds32Dr.png",
    "status": true
}
